blockingIssues.description: The command-palette action labeled "Rescan all skills" still does not run Discover's filesystem scan. `AppShell` now awaits `platformStore.rescan()` and then calls `discoverStore.refreshCounts()`, but `refreshCounts()` only reloads persisted `get_discovered_skills` rows while the real disk scan path remains `startScan()` / `start_project_scan`. Central reloads and the live Claude refresh were preserved, but Discover can still stay stale for new or removed project skills.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/claude-source-aware-detail-api.md` to record that the Claude AI explanation surface is now row-aware, so duplicate `user` and `marketplace` rows keep explanation state separate by selected row identity.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add or point to a reproducible native Tauri automation recipe for command-palette/global-search flows (isolated HOME launch, accessibility steps, and evidence capture) so workers do not keep rediscovering ad hoc macOS automation for this surface.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `global-rescan-surface-regression-fix` review still found native verification relying on ad hoc accessibility scripting, while the current worker skill says `.factory/library/user-testing.md` is the canonical recipe even though that library entry does not yet cover command-palette/global-search interaction details.
